data.body:
MANUAL PLAN-REVIEW of epic 8f6c (code-grounding evidence oracle, v1 T0+T1) using the 5fd2 gate's REAL
criteria/prompts/process. Goal: feedback on the epic, not to implement the gate.

== PROCESS (faithful to 5fd2 / three_pass.py) ==
Target = 8f6c (EPIC, with 6 children). applies_at filtered to the epic-level + container subset (20 criteria:
F1 F4 E1 E2 E3 G5 COH T5e T2 T3 T4 T7 T12 T1 T8 T10 T11 G3 G4 G6; ISF n/a — no session-log link; leaf criteria
E6/G1G2/A1/T5a-c/T6/T9/E5/T5d correctly dropped at epic altitude). Ran the real three-pass:
- Pass 1 (find): 3 independent finder agents by facet (plan-text judgment / agent-tier codebase-grounded /
  container parent-vs-children), using the verbatim PASS1_SYSTEM contract + the v8 scenario+checklist rubric,
  over the complete artifact (epic + 6 children + decision record) with repo + ticket-graph access.
- Pass 2 (verify): ONE separate verifier, fresh context, verbatim PASS2_SYSTEM (charitable plan-reading;
  absence findings held to the complete-artifact bar; repo-grounded for code citations). Emits per finding the
  coarse severity attributes + the typed binary sub-answer set.
- Pass 3 (decide): deterministic pass3_decide (graded-fraction confidence; ordinal severity; advisory-by-default
  v1 — only the DET floor blocks). Computed exactly, no model judgment in the path.

== DET FLOOR (P1-P8) == PASS / fail-open.
P1 readiness-shape PASS (## Acceptance Criteria, 6 lines). P2 file/symbol/import resolution SKIPPED — fail-open
(the grounding tooling it needs IS this epic; nicely meta). P3 package-existence: named tools (OpenGrep,
grep-ast, ctags, syft, scc, lizard, ast-grep) are real → PASS (note: not yet in pyproject — proposed deps).
P4 oversize: large epic but decomposed into 6 children → addressed. P5 task-DAG: valid (S1→S2/S3/S4→S5/S6, no
cycle); cross-child note — all children add modules under one new src/rebar/grounding/ package. P6 AC/DD quality:
present (see F1 finding re conjunctive lines). P7 destructive: none (additive, optional) PASS. P8 reviewability:
within budget PASS. NET: DET floor does not block.

== RESULT == 10 Pass-1 findings -> 6 ADVISORY (survived verification) + 4 DROPPED (Pass-2 conf<0.5). 0 BLOCKS
(correct: v1 advisory-only; the DET floor, the only blocking tier, passed). Strong cross-finder convergence on
ONE theme: the load-bearing reuse/capability bets were never empirically de-risked. The gate found real things.

== ADVISORY FINDINGS (ranked; coaching form) ==
1. conf=1.00 MAJOR [T2 empirical-probe / G5] — The epic's value rests on an UNVALIDATED assumption: that the
   grep-ast/ctags T1 floor can REFUTE a useful fraction of hallucinated-symbol claims polyglot, rather than
   abstaining so often it is near-useless. S6 conformance tests fail-open (abstain/coverage) but never the
   refutation YIELD. No spike measures yield before the full 6-child build. (Two finders converged: T2 and T3.)
   FIX: add an early spike (in/before S2) measuring refute-rate vs abstain-rate on a small representative
   polyglot corpus; state a target yield. CHECK: [ ] spike measures T1-floor refutation yield before S2-S6 commit.
2. conf=0.86 MAJOR [T3 feasibility / T1] — The two highest-risk reuse bets are unverified capability assertions:
   (a) OpenGrep's project-local rule-loading is rich enough to BE the registry substrate (the "do NOT re-invent a
   match DSL" decision rests entirely on this), and (b) grep-ast yields a polyglot REPO-WIDE cross-file symbol
   index (existing src/rebar/llm/code_review.py:10 calls a full tree-sitter/PageRank repo-map "a future"). All
   named tools are absent from src/ + pyproject (verified) — genuinely new integrations, no spike anywhere.
   FIX: a 2-language spike proving (a) OpenGrep loads+namespaces a .rebar/detectors/ rule and (b) grep-ast/ctags
   returns a cross-file repo-wide symbol hit, BEFORE locking S2/S4. CHECK: [ ] both capability bets spiked.
3. conf=0.86 MAJOR [G6 mechanism-correctness] — S1's fail-open harness is specified as "mirror acli_subprocess:
   process-group, SIGTERM->SIGKILL" — but that is a SUBPROCESS reaper (verified acli_subprocess.py:411 uses
   Popen(start_new_session=True)+os.killpg). grep-ast/tree-sitter run IN-PROCESS (pip C-extensions); a hung parse
   can't be interrupted by a thread/signal timeout and a segfault kills the host interpreter, not a child. So the
   IRONCLAD "crash/timeout->abstain, never raises" invariant is unachievable for in-process backends by the named
   mechanism. FIX: S1 must either run ALL backends behind a subprocess boundary (uniform reaper) or specify a
   separate, explicitly-bounded in-process strategy. CHECK: [ ] S1 harness covers in-process backend crash/hang.
4. conf=0.71 MAJOR [G5 decomposition] — Horizontal big-bang: S5 (public API/consumer contract) and S6
   (conformance) both depend on ALL THREE engines (S2,S3,S4); no thin vertical slice proves the contract
   end-to-end (one language, one job, harness->consumer) until every engine is done, concentrating
   integration/contract-fitness risk at the end. FIX: carve a thin vertical slice (S1 + minimal Engine A + the
   public API + a consumer smoke) early. CHECK: [ ] a thin end-to-end slice precedes the full engine build-out.
5. conf=0.57 MAJOR [G4 child-consistency] — version_skew is tested by S4 (AC4) and S6 (AC1) and the epic calls it
   "the #1 real failure", but it is ABSENT from S1's enumerated reason enum (only a trailing "…"). Risk: children
   coin divergent reason tokens; a consumer has no enum case for the #1 failure. FIX: add version_skew to S1's
   enumerated reason enum. CHECK: [ ] version_skew is in the S1 reason enum.
6. conf=0.50 MAJOR [T5e maintainability] — scc/lizard metric thresholds (oversize/too-complex cutoffs for the
   smell router + plan-review P4) are a new evolving rule but never stated configurable; only per-detector
   severity/enable deltas are. Risk: hardcoded cutoffs -> noisy router, not tunable. FIX: make metric cutoffs
   project-configurable in S4. CHECK: [ ] metric thresholds are configurable.

== DROPPED BY VERIFICATION (Pass-2 conf<0.5; informative, not actioned) ==
- [F1] conjunctive ACs (4-6 outcomes/checkbox), conf .43 — real but child ACs (S3/S4) decompose the same
  outcomes into separately-checkable bullets where close happens; partial mitigation exists.
- [F1] "dependency root for the rest" non-observable clause, conf .29 — charitable reading: S1's own ACs restate
  the work observably; the clause is descriptive framing.
- [T8] open reason enum + under-specified reference-in 'kind', conf .29 — verifier judged enum/kind enumeration a
  leaf-spec detail S1/S2 legitimately close at implementation (epic-altitude deferral charitable). (NB: finding 5
  re version_skew survived; the broader "enum is open" did not.)
- [G4] reference-in schema double-owned S2 vs S5, conf .29 — verifier read the verbs as an intentional split
  (S2 "defined+validated" vs S5 "documented"), no genuine ownership fork.

== AFFIRMATIONS (what passed cleanly — per the coaching spec) ==
- T1 prior-art/NIH: PASS — reuse targets all real/new, alternatives rejected with reasons, deep prior-art sweep,
  novelty justified. The epic passed the very criterion designed to catch reinvention/Frankenstein.
- T4 compat/destructiveness: n/a (purely additive, optional, non-adopting clients unaffected).
- T10 infra / T11 migration: n/a (library; no IaC; no persisted-schema/data migration).
- G3 child coverage: all 6 epic ACs map 1:1 fully to children — no uncovered/partial AC.
- E3 intent fidelity: title "v1: T0+T1 floor" faithfully scopes T2 out. F4: named consumers (5fd2/9da1).

== META == The gate's highest-confidence findings cluster on exactly what such a gate exists to catch: missing
empirical de-risking of novel/load-bearing assumptions (T2/T3), a mechanism-correctness gap (G6), and
decomposition sequencing (G5) — all genuine, cheap-to-address improvements. Recommended next: fold findings
1-6 into the epic/children (an early spike story or AC; S1 in-process-harness AC; version_skew in the enum;
S4 configurable thresholds; a thin vertical slice), then re-run if desired.
